script_model_response:
Perfect! I've completed your road trip research. Here's what I found:

## Your Colorado Camping Destination

**Park:** Rocky Mountain National Park (near Estes Park, CO)
- **Why this park?** It has the most visitor centers (10) of any national park in Colorado with hiking and camping activities
- **Campground:** Moraine Park Campground
- **Why this campground?** It has the largest total number of campsites (292) in the park:
  - 155 tent-only sites
  - 88 RV-only sites  
  - 49 walk/boat-to sites
  - 49 sites with electrical hookups

## Driving Details from Denver, CO

**Distance:** 71.5 miles  
**Estimated Travel Time:** 1 hour 37 minutes

**Route Summary:** Take I-25 N to US-36 W (Denver Boulder Turnpike) through Boulder and Lyons, then continue on US-36 W into Estes Park and Rocky Mountain National Park. Turn onto Bear Lake Road, then right onto Kaley Cottage Rd W to reach Moraine Park Campground.

**Note:** Moraine Park Campground is currently open for winter camping (first-come, first-served through May 18, 2026). For summer 2026, reservations are required and can be made at www.recreation.gov. The campground offers both tent and RV camping with amenities including flush toilets (seasonal), potable water, food storage lockers, and a dump station.
